flags.concern: Effect drag UX: Step 4 targets the wrong surface for drag creation. In this repo, the named `EffectCreatorPanel` is a modal effect editor, not a persistent palette users can drag from, so that step does not actually implement the requested drag-from-effect-creator workflow.
flags.evidence: `/Users/peteromalley/Documents/reigh-workspace/reigh-app/src/tools/video-editor/components/PropertiesPanel/ClipPanel.tsx:391-407` is the only mount of `EffectCreatorPanel`, and `/Users/peteromalley/Documents/reigh-workspace/reigh-app/src/tools/video-editor/components/EffectCreatorPanel.tsx:6-12,41-49,166-172` shows it is a `Dialog`-based editor opened from clip properties. The existing drag-source pattern is instead the footer tool in `/Users/peteromalley/Documents/reigh-workspace/reigh-app/src/tools/video-editor/components/TimelineEditor/TimelineCanvas.tsx:560-601`.

flags.concern: Preview overlay editing: The plan misses the preview overlay path. If an effect-layer clip can be selected, the current overlay editor will treat it like a visual clip and show a full-frame move/crop box, allowing invalid geometry edits on a clip that should have no spatial bounds.
flags.evidence: `/Users/peteromalley/Documents/reigh-workspace/reigh-app/src/tools/video-editor/components/PreviewPanel/OverlayEditor.tsx:167-200` falls back to track-sized bounds for any non-text clip without explicit geometry, and `/Users/peteromalley/Documents/reigh-workspace/reigh-app/src/tools/video-editor/components/PreviewPanel/OverlayEditor.tsx:220-244` includes the selected clip even when it has no position overrides. Step 9 only narrows tabs in `ClipPanel`; it does not cover this editor.

flags.concern: Timeline compositing order: The plan claims overlapping effect layers on the same track should work, but the current preview renderer has no deterministic within-track z-order beyond start time. Without carrying clip order into rendering or forbidding same-track overlap, stacked same-track effect layers will compose incidentally rather than by a defined rule.
flags.evidence: `/Users/peteromalley/Documents/reigh-workspace/reigh-app/src/tools/video-editor/lib/timeline-data.ts:58-72,146-175` maintains `clipOrder` in editor state, but `/Users/peteromalley/Documents/reigh-workspace/reigh-app/src/tools/video-editor/compositions/TimelineRenderer.tsx:9-10,18,27-32,81-96` groups clips only from `config.clips` and sorts them only by `at`. The render path never receives or uses `clipOrder`.

flags.concern: Properties panel timing: Step 9 narrows effect-layer clips to `effects` and `timing`, but it does not replace the current media-oriented timing controls. As written, effect-layer clips would still show `speed` / `from` / `to` inputs instead of a hold-duration control, which misses the plan's own success criterion about showing only relevant controls.
flags.evidence: `/Users/peteromalley/Documents/reigh-workspace/reigh-app/src/tools/video-editor/components/PropertiesPanel/ClipPanel.tsx:40-50` only changes which tabs appear, while `/Users/peteromalley/Documents/reigh-workspace/reigh-app/src/tools/video-editor/components/PropertiesPanel/ClipPanel.tsx:411-429` hard-codes the timing tab to `Start`, `Speed`, `Source In`, and `Source Out`.
